terms.definition: A state-coordinated system in which social and economic risks are pooled across the citizenry and mitigated through centralized policy instruments.
A governance regime whose primary normative commitment is the permanent mitigation of systemic risk to its citizens via large-scale administrative structures.
The socially recognized center from which legitimate moral authority and judgment are believed to flow.
A qualitative systemic change triggered when a control parameter crosses a critical threshold, borrowed analogically from statistical physics.
A social order in which formally credentialed knowledge and expertise constitute the primary basis for status and authority.
A structural similarity across systems or institutions that allows patterns in one domain to map onto another.
terms.first_use_section: Media — The Hays Code as Re-Moralization

terms.definition: The science and policy practice of preventing disease and promoting health at the population level through organized efforts.
A household unit composed of two married parents and their biological or adopted children living independently.
terms.first_use_section: Family Structure — The Plunge in Home Ownership

terms.definition: An economic system evaluated not merely by efficiency but by its perceived fairness and capacity to uphold communal moral norms.
terms.first_use_section: Family Structure — The Plunge in Home Ownership
